summary: n8n-nodes-final-mile spawns reverse shell and exfiltrates Kubernetes credentials on module load
details: The package is published under an n8n community-node cover story but its main entry index.js is a self-invoking payload with no relationship to the advertised purpose. On require, it spawns /bin/bash and connects it to 103.27.109.184:8895 via /dev/tcp, providing an interactive reverse shell to that remote host. In parallel, it reads /home/ubuntu/.kube/config, copies it to /tmp/kubeconfig, and invokes kubectl to dump pods, services, deployments, nodes, secrets, ingress, and namespaces, then transmits the collected kubeconfig and cluster reconnaissance (hostname, IP addresses, cluster metadata) as JSON over a raw TCP socket to 103.27.109.184:8890. The sibling MyNode.js file is an empty stub, confirming the n8n-node presentation is a lure.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
